description_long: This guard stone is a roughly hewn, weathered stone object with an irregular, organic shape. The base is broad and squat, with a rounded, slightly flattened top section that rises from the center, resembling a worn pillar or post. The surface is mottled with shades of gray, brown, and off-white, showing signs of significant aging and exposure to the elements. The stone appears to have natural pitting, chipping, and discoloration, likely from years of contact with vehicles, foot traffic, and weather. Its form is asymmetrical, with one side slightly more protruding and the top section tapering unevenly. The overall appearance suggests it was hand-carved or minimally shaped, typical of historical or utilitarian urban barriers.
description: A weathered, irregularly shaped stone guard stone with a broad base and rounded top, showing signs of age and exposure. Its mottled surface and asymmetrical form suggest long-term use as a protective barrier.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broadest face with a rounded top and visible surface pitting and discoloration.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the tapering of the top section and the uneven, organic profile of the stone.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the rounded back and the mottled, aged surface with less pronounced features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the irregular base and the sloping, asymmetrical rise of the top section.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, at the entrance to driveways, or near building facades to protect structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be partially embedded in the ground with the broad base flush against the surface and the rounded top exposed above ground.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and smoothing on the edges and corners, with surface discoloration and erosion likely caused by repeated vehicle contact, weather exposure, and occasional foot traffic. The top and protruding sides show the most wear.
